pattern: BFS simulation: open reachable boxes whenever you have them and hold a key, collecting candies and chaining newly discovered boxes and keys.
hints: Use a queue of boxes you currently hold but haven't opened; process a box only if its status is open or you've found its key.
When you open a box, collect its candies, add contained boxes to your held set, and mark newly discovered keys; those keys may unblock previously held but locked boxes.
Keep a set of boxes you hold and a set of keys you own; re-enqueue a held box the moment you acquire its key.

edge_inputs.description: example 2: one box unlocks all others
all boxes closed with no keys reachable
key discovered inside a closed box, need the key to get the key
single open box with all candies
cycle: box 0 contains box 1, box 1 contains key 0, but box 0 starts open

stress.description: Random n=100 scenario constructed to be fully reachable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    status = [1] * n
    candies = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    keys = [[] for _ in range(n)]
    containedBoxes = [[] for _ in range(n)]
    return [status, candies, keys, containedBoxes, list(range(n))]
